skill.name: slack-automation
skill.description: Automate Slack messaging, channel management, search, reactions, and threads via Rube MCP (Composio). Send messages, search conversations, manage channels/users, and react to messages programmatically.
skill.summary: Automate Slack messaging, search, threads, reactions, scheduling, and workspace lookup through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static shell-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown code spans, Slack tool names, and words such as desc. The confirmed risk is legitimate but sensitive networked Slack automation through Rube MCP, including message posting, searching, user listing, reactions, and scheduling after OAuth authorization.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Slack Workspace Mutations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to send, edit, schedule, thread, and react to Slack messages through an authorized Rube MCP Slack connection. This is expected functionality, but misuse or overly broad OAuth scopes could create unwanted workspace changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool sequences explicitly include Slack send, edit, schedule, and reaction actions. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it can modify Slack state when authorized.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Slack Data Retrieval
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to search messages, fetch threads, list users, list channels, and retrieve workspace metadata. These actions can expose sensitive workspace content within the granted Slack scopes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited workflows clearly retrieve messages, threads, users, channels, and team metadata. The risk depends on granted OAuth scopes, but the data access capability is direct and intentional.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code spans around Slack and Rube tool names, parameters, and response fields. No executable script, shell command, or Ruby code was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching characters appear in Markdown prose and table entries, not code blocks or executable files. The skill contains only SKILL.md documentation and no runnable Ruby or shell script.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high cryptography findings map to ordinary words or Slack parameters, and the reconnaissance findings map to documented Slack response fields and validation text. No cryptographic implementation, system probing, or local host enumeration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language descriptions, Slack API parameters, or quick-reference table rows. I found no hashing, encryption, process discovery, network scanning, or system reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Is Explicit and User Configured
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to configure the Rube MCP endpoint. This is a real network dependency, but it is not hidden and there is no evidence of covert exfiltration in the skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is visible setup documentation for the required MCP server. Network use is intentional, but no secret harvesting or concealed outbound destination was found.

content.value_statement: Slack operations often require repeated channel lookup, message search, and manual follow-up. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for reliable Slack automation.
actual_capabilities: Resolve Slack channel names and user names before taking action.
Send, edit, thread, and schedule Slack messages through Rube MCP.
Search Slack messages and fetch related conversation threads.
List channels, users, user groups, and workspace metadata available to the authorized connection.
Add, remove, and inspect emoji reactions on Slack messages.
Handle pagination, rate limits, Slack IDs, and common response nesting issues.
limitations: Requires a connected Rube MCP server and an active Slack OAuth connection.
Available actions depend on Slack scopes granted to the connected account or app.
Private channels and direct messages may be unavailable unless the app has access.
Slack search can lag behind recently posted messages.

use_cases.title: Post Release Updates
use_cases.description: Resolve the release channel, send a formatted update, and keep the message timestamp for later edits or threaded replies.

use_cases.title: Find Customer Escalation Threads
use_cases.description: Search Slack by keywords, channel, author, and date range, then fetch full threads for context.

use_cases.title: Audit Workspace Participation
use_cases.description: List channels, users, user groups, and team metadata to support workspace reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Channel Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Slack automation skill to find #announcements and send this message: Team standup moves to 10:30 tomorrow.

prompt_templates.title: Search and Summarize Threads
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Slack for messages about billing outage after 2026-06-01, fetch relevant threads, and summarize the active action items.

prompt_templates.title: Schedule a Reminder
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve #customer-success and schedule a formatted reminder for next Monday at 9:00 AM UTC.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the incident channel, locate the latest database thread, add an eyes reaction, and post a concise status reply in the thread.

output_examples.input: Send a formatted launch reminder to #product-launch.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the channel, posts the reminder with Markdown formatting, and reports the Slack timestamp for follow-up edits.

output_examples.input: Find recent messages from a support lead about refund delays.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches by author and keywords, fetches matching threads, and returns a concise summary with channel names and dates.

output_examples.input: React to the latest deployment announcement.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches for the announcement, verifies the channel and timestamp, then adds the requested emoji reaction.

best_practices: Resolve channel and user IDs before sending messages or reactions.
Confirm destination, content, and timing before mutating Slack state.
Paginate list and search results until the cursor is empty.
anti_patterns: Do not post to a channel name without resolving and confirming the channel ID.
Do not assume private channels are searchable unless the app has access.
Do not ignore Slack rate limits or Retry-After guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill need a Slack token in the prompt?
faq.answer: No. It expects Slack access to be managed through an active Rube MCP connection.

faq.question: Can it send direct messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can resolve users and open or reuse a direct message channel when scopes allow it.

faq.question: Can it read private channels?
faq.answer: Only when the connected Slack app or account has access to those conversations.

faq.question: Can it edit messages after posting?
faq.answer: Yes. It must keep the original channel ID and message timestamp from the send response.

faq.question: Does it support scheduled messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents Slack scheduled messages with a Unix timestamp and Slack delivery limits.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive workspaces?
faq.answer: Use least-privilege Slack scopes and require confirmation before posting, editing, or scheduling messages.
